data.title: Spark New Zealand Ltd.: FY25 EBITDAI Guidance Cut to NZ$1.04B to NZ$1.1B
SEI INVESTMENTS CO SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
SEI Investments Company, a leading provider of technology, operations, and asset management services, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the competitive financial services industry. SEI's focus on innovation, operational efficiency, and strategic acquisitions has positioned it well for future growth.

Financial Highlights

SEI Investments Company reported strong financial results for 2024, showcasing significant growth across key metrics:

  • Total Revenues: $2,125.2 million, reflecting an 11% increase from the previous year, driven by higher assets under administration and the launch of the SEI Integrated Cash Program.
  • Income from Operations: $551.7 million, a 30% increase, attributed to revenue growth and cost containment measures.
  • Net Income: $581.2 million, a 26% increase, supported by higher revenues and improved operational efficiency.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $4.41, up 27% from the previous year, reflecting strong financial performance and share repurchases.

Business Highlights

SEI Investments Company operates through four core client-oriented business segments: Investment Managers, Private Banks, Investment Advisors, and Institutional Investors. Each segment contributes to the company's revenue through distinct services such as investment management, technology and operations outsourcing, and professional services.

  • Revenue Segments: The Investment Managers segment saw a 13% increase in revenues in 2024, driven by additional services to alternative fund clients and positive cash flows. The Private Banks segment experienced a 9% revenue increase due to new SWP client conversions and market appreciation. The Investment Advisors segment's revenues grew by 17% in 2024, largely due to the SEI Integrated Cash Program and increased fees from separately managed account programs.
  • Geographical Performance: SEI has a global presence with service centers in the United States, United Kingdom, Ireland, Canada, continental Europe, India, and South Africa. The company serves a diverse client base, including 8 of the top 20 U.S. banks and 43 of the top 100 investment managers worldwide.
  • New Product Launches: In December 2023, SEI launched the SEI Integrated Cash Program, an enhanced cash sweep program that contributed significantly to the revenue of the Investment Advisors segment in 2024.
  • Operational Efficiency: SEI continues to invest in technology and operational infrastructure to enhance efficiency and performance. This includes the SEI Wealth Platform, which integrates technology, operational outsourcing, and asset management.
  • Future Outlook: SEI plans to continue its focus on technology enhancements and operational infrastructure improvements to support business growth and client needs. The company is also exploring new global markets and emphasizing defined contribution and not-for-profit organizations within its Institutional Investors segment.

Strategic Initiatives

SEI Investments Company pursued several strategic initiatives to enhance its capabilities and scale:

  • Acquisitions: The company acquired LifeYield in 2024 and XPS Pensions (Nexus) Limited and Altigo in 2023 to bolster its service offerings and market presence.
  • Infrastructure Investments: SEI focused on improving technology and operational efficiency, including automating processes and enhancing cybersecurity systems.
  • Capital Management: In 2024, SEI repurchased 6.8 million shares of its common stock at an average price of $74.92 per share, totaling $512.5 million, and paid $120.3 million in cash dividends. The company maintained a credit facility with borrowings up to $325 million, with $320.1 million available for corporate purposes as of January 31, 2025. SEI also increased its capitalized software development costs to $24.3 million in 2024, focusing on enhancements to the SEI Wealth Platform.
  • Future Outlook: SEI plans to continue investing in technology infrastructure to support long-term growth and maintain competitive advantages. The company anticipates that its operating cash flow, available borrowing capacity, and existing cash and cash equivalents will be sufficient to meet operational needs and fund the stock repurchase program for at least the next 12 months. SEI also expects to recognize $28.6 million in amortization expense related to capitalized software development costs in 2025.

Challenges and Risks

SEI Investments Company faces several challenges and risks in the competitive financial services industry:

  • Market Risks: The company's revenues and earnings are sensitive to changes in capital markets and the value of financial instruments. Market volatility, geopolitical events, and economic downturns can adversely affect asset values and client investment decisions.
  • Operational Risks: The reliance on third-party service providers and the implementation of a new outsourcing strategy in India introduce risks related to operational efficiency, regulatory compliance, and cybersecurity. The complexity of integrating new technologies and managing global operations poses additional challenges.
  • Regulatory Risks: SEI is subject to extensive regulations across multiple jurisdictions, which can impact its operations and ability to offer certain products. Compliance with evolving regulations, such as those related to anti-money laundering and data protection, is critical.
  • Emerging Risks: The rise of alternative investments and the demand for hyper-personalized solutions present both opportunities and challenges. The company must effectively integrate these offerings to avoid reputational damage and financial losses.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: The increasing sophistication of cyber-attacks and the potential for data breaches pose significant threats to SEI's operations and reputation. The company must continuously enhance its cybersecurity measures to protect sensitive information.

Management acknowledges the challenges posed by increased competition and regulatory scrutiny. The company is focusing on enhancing its technology platforms and expanding its global footprint to mitigate these risks. Strategic investments in innovation and talent acquisition are key components of SEI's response to the evolving market landscape.

SEC Filing: SEI INVESTMENTS CO [ SEIC ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: PPG INDUSTRIES INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
PPG Industries Inc., a global leader in paints, coatings, and specialty materials, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report highlights the company's efforts to navigate a competitive market, manage costs, and drive innovation while addressing global economic uncertainties.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $15,845 million, a decrease of 2% compared to the prior year, primarily due to lower sales volumes and unfavorable foreign currency translation.
  • Income Before Income Taxes: $1,852 million, an increase of $162 million compared to the prior year, driven by lower raw material costs and restructuring savings.
  • Net Income (attributable to PPG): $1,116 million, reflecting a decrease from the prior year due to a loss from discontinued operations.
  • Earnings per Diluted Share, Continuing Operations: $5.72, an increase of 10.9% from the prior year, supported by cost reductions and efficiency improvements.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: PPG's business is divided into three main segments: Global Architectural Coatings, Performance Coatings, and Industrial Coatings. Each segment serves different markets and customer needs, with specific product lines such as paints, coatings, adhesives, and sealants.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates globally with significant non-U.S. operations, which helps mitigate economic impacts in any single region. In 2024, sales volumes were modestly higher in the Asia Pacific and Latin America regions, while demand in Europe was soft, particularly affecting the automotive OEM coatings and architectural coatings businesses.
  • Sales Units: The Performance Coatings segment saw a 2% increase in net sales driven by higher selling prices and sales volumes, particularly in aerospace coatings, which experienced double-digit growth due to strong demand and customer order backlogs.
  • New Product Launches: PPG continued to innovate with sustainably-advantaged solutions, including compact automotive paint processes, low temperature cure capabilities, and coatings for recyclable metal packaging, contributing to 41% of sales from sustainably-advantaged products.
  • New Production Launches: The company focused on debottlenecking and expanding manufacturing capabilities in aerospace coatings to drive volume and earnings growth.
  • Future Outlook: PPG anticipates a slow start to 2025 with challenges in Europe and global industrial markets but expects growth in China, India, and Mexico. The company plans to deliver low single-digit organic sales growth, with continued strength in aerospace coatings and protective and marine coatings.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: In 2024, PPG executed strategic divestitures, including the sale of its silicas products business and the architectural coatings business in the U.S. and Canada, to improve its financial profile and focus on core operations. The company also approved a comprehensive cost reduction program aimed at reducing structural costs, primarily in Europe, with anticipated annualized pre-tax savings of approximately $175 million.
  • Capital Management: PPG repurchased approximately 5.8 million shares for $752 million in 2024, with $2.8 billion remaining under the current share repurchase authorization. The company increased its quarterly dividend by approximately 5% to $0.68 per share, marking the 53rd consecutive year of increased annual per-share dividend payments. PPG also issued a €500 million term loan due in 2026 and repaid $300 million of long-term debt.
  • Future Outlook: PPG anticipates a slow start to 2025 due to continued demand challenges in Europe and global industrial markets. However, the company expects low single-digit organic sales growth, driven by demand growth in China, India, and Mexico, and continued strength in aerospace coatings. PPG plans to maintain its focus on cost management and strategic investments to support sustainable growth, with capital expenditures projected to be between $725 million and $775 million in 2025.

Challenges and Risks

  • Competitive Industry: PPG operates in a highly competitive coatings industry, facing competition from both global and regional firms. The divestiture of its architectural coatings business in the U.S. and Canada may impact its market presence in these regions.
  • Global Operations Risks: PPG's global operations expose it to risks related to differing legal, political, social, and regulatory requirements, as well as economic conditions in various countries.
  • Economic Risks: The company faces several economic risks, including fluctuations in raw material prices and availability, which could negatively impact financial results. Foreign currency exchange rate risks could also affect its financial condition and results of operations.
  • Public Health and Regulatory Risks: Public health crises, such as pandemics, could disrupt operations and supply chains. Regulatory risks include evolving environmental laws and potential liabilities from lawsuits and claims.
  • Geopolitical Uncertainties: PPG's international operations are subject to geopolitical uncertainties, including tensions between major economies and ongoing conflicts.
  • Management Strategies: In response to these challenges, PPG has implemented cost reduction programs to improve profitability, with expected savings of $175 million annually. The company is also focused on managing raw material costs and improving manufacturing efficiencies.

SEC Filing: PPG INDUSTRIES INC [ PPG ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: HF Sinclair Corp SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
HF Sinclair Corp, a leading energy company with operations spanning refining, renewables, marketing, lubricants & specialties, and midstream, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Sales and other revenues: $28,580 million, decreased 11% from the previous year due to decreased refined product sales prices and lower excess crude oil sales volumes.

Gross margin: $58 million, significantly lower than the previous year due to decreased sales prices and lower crude oil and feedstock prices.

Income from operations: $261 million, a decrease from $2,203 million in the previous year, primarily driven by lower adjusted refinery gross margins.

Net income attributable to HF Sinclair stockholders: $177 million, a decrease of $1,413 million compared to the previous year, driven by lower adjusted refinery gross margins.

Earnings per share attributable to HF Sinclair stockholders: $0.91, down from $8.29 in the previous year, reflecting the decrease in net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: HF Sinclair's operations are organized into five reportable segments: Refining, Renewables, Marketing, Lubricants & Specialties, and Midstream. Each segment contributes to the company's overall performance with distinct operational focuses.

Geographical Performance: The company operates refineries in Kansas, Oklahoma, New Mexico, Wyoming, Washington, and Utah, with a marketing focus in the Southwest United States, the Rocky Mountains, and the Pacific Northwest. The Mid-Continent and West regions experienced lower refining margins in 2024 due to high global supply of transportation fuels.

Sales Units: In 2024, the company reported sales of 637,170 barrels per day of produced refined products, with the Mid-Continent region processing 251,650 barrels per day and the West region processing 350,430 barrels per day.

Sales Units - Renewables: The Renewables segment saw increased sales volumes, with 255,639 thousand gallons of produced renewables products sold in 2024.

Sales Units - Marketing: The Marketing segment reported sales of 1,376,291 thousand gallons of refined products in 2024, with a strong performance from Sinclair branded sites.

Sales Units - Lubricants & Specialties: The Lubricants & Specialties segment sold 32,100 barrels per day of produced refined products in 2024.

New Production Launches: The company produces renewable diesel at two facilities in Wyoming and one in New Mexico, highlighting its commitment to expanding renewable energy production.

Future Outlook: For the first quarter of 2025, HF Sinclair expects to run between 580,000-620,000 barrels per day of crude oil, reflecting planned turnarounds at the Tulsa refinery. The company also anticipates continued weakness in RINs and LCFS prices, impacting renewable diesel margins.

Future Outlook - Marketing: HF Sinclair plans to grow the number of branded sites by approximately 10% annually, leveraging the strong value of Sinclair branded sites.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: HF Sinclair Corporation has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its operational and financial flexibility. The company completed the HEP Merger Transaction, integrating HEP as an indirect, wholly owned subsidiary, which is expected to streamline operations and improve synergies. Additionally, HF Sinclair has focused on optimizing its existing assets and selectively acquiring complementary assets to increase earnings and cash flow. The company is also investing in projects that improve operational reliability and profitability, including enhancements to refinery processing capabilities and production yield.

Capital Management: HF Sinclair has been actively managing its capital through a disciplined approach. The company authorized a $1.0 billion share repurchase program in August 2023, which was followed by a new $1.0 billion share repurchase program in May 2024. Under these programs, HF Sinclair repurchased 11,944,177 shares for $664 million in 2024. The company also declared a regular quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, payable in March 2025. In terms of debt management, HF Sinclair completed a senior notes exchange and issued new senior notes totaling $1.4 billion to refinance existing debt and fund strategic initiatives. The company maintains a $1.65 billion senior unsecured revolving credit facility and a $1.2 billion senior secured revolving credit facility to support its liquidity needs.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, HF Sinclair plans to continue its focus on strategic investments and capital projects that drive long-term value. The company expects to self-fund development projects and make strategic investments focused on profitable growth while reducing debt and returning cash to stockholders through dividends and share repurchases. HF Sinclair's expected capital and turnaround cash spending for 2025 is projected to be $875 million, with a focus on sustaining and growth capital investments. The company also aims to maintain financial flexibility to execute its capital priorities and generate long-term value for its stockholders.

Challenges and Risks

Market Risks: The company faces significant market risks due to the fluctuating prices of crude oil, renewable feedstocks, and refined products, which are influenced by factors beyond its control, such as global economic conditions, geopolitical tensions, and regulatory changes. The profitability of its segments is highly dependent on the spread between market prices for refined products and crude oil, as well as renewable diesel and feedstocks.

Operational Risks: The company is exposed to operational risks, including catastrophic losses, unforeseen interruptions, and inadequate insurance coverage, which could materially affect its operations and financial condition.

Regulatory Risks: The company faces regulatory risks, with significant costs and liabilities arising from compliance with environmental, health, and safety laws. New regulations, such as those related to GHG emissions and climate change, could increase operating costs and reduce demand for the company's products. The introduction of tariffs and changes in trade policies could further impact the cost structure and availability of raw materials, affecting the company's competitive position.

Emerging Risks: Increasing attention to ESG matters may impact the company's business and financial results. The investment community's focus on ESG practices could lead to increased costs, reduced demand for products, and challenges in accessing capital. Additionally, the company may face litigation risks related to climate change and environmental issues, which could result in significant financial and reputational damage.

Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to adapt to changing market conditions and regulatory environments as a key challenge. Strategies to address these risks include diversifying supply sources, enhancing operational efficiencies, and investing in technologies to reduce emissions and improve sustainability. The company is also focused on maintaining strong relationships with distributors and customers to mitigate market risks and ensure a stable demand for its products.

Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to market risks related to fluctuations in commodity prices, interest rates, and foreign currency exchange rates. These risks could adversely affect the company's financial results and cash flows. To manage these risks, the company employs hedging strategies and closely monitors market conditions to adjust its operations and financial strategies accordingly.

SEC Filing: HF Sinclair Corp [ DINO ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: Bunge Global SA SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Bunge Global SA, a leading global agribusiness and food company, has released its annual 10-K report, providing a comprehensive overview of its financial and operational performance for the year ended December 31, 2024. The report details the company's financial metrics, business highlights, strategic initiatives, and the challenges and risks it faces in the volatile agribusiness and food industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $52,054 million, reflecting a decrease due to lower average sales prices and volumes across segments.
  • Gross Profit: $3,422 million, a decrease driven by lower margins and foreign exchange losses.
  • Total EBIT: $1,792 million, primarily impacted by lower Core Segment EBIT and foreign exchange losses.
  • Net Income Attributable to Bunge Shareholders: $1,137 million, a decrease due to lower Core Segment EBIT and foreign exchange losses.
  • Diluted EPS: $7.99 per share, reflecting a decrease from the previous year due to lower net income.

Business Highlights

  • [Revenue Segments - Agribusiness]: The Agribusiness segment experienced a 10% decrease in net sales to $38,598 million, primarily due to lower average sales prices and volumes in global soybean oilseed processing businesses, offset by higher volumes in South America and Europe.
  • [Revenue Segments - Refined and Specialty Oils]: Net sales in the Refined and Specialty Oils segment decreased by 13% to $12,771 million, driven by lower average sales prices across all regions and uncertainty related to U.S. biofuel policies, with increased volumes in Asia and North America.
  • [Revenue Segments - Milling]: The Milling segment saw an 18% decrease in net sales to $1,555 million, attributed to lower sales prices in South American wheat milling and North American corn milling businesses, despite increased volumes.
  • [Revenue Segments - Sugar and Bioenergy]: The Sugar and Bioenergy segment's net sales decreased by 45% to $130 million, with a significant EBIT increase due to a $195 million gain from the sale of Bunge's 50% ownership in BP Bunge Bioenergia.
  • [Geographical Performance - South America]: South America saw increased volumes in the Agribusiness segment due to the non-recurrence of the prior year's drought in Argentina, contributing positively to the segment's performance.
  • [Geographical Performance - Europe]: The European region experienced higher volumes in the softseed business, driven by increased activity at Ukrainian facilities, despite overall lower average sales prices.
  • [Sales Units - Agribusiness]: Agribusiness segment volumes increased by 6% to 80,628 thousand metric tons, reflecting higher activity levels in certain regions.
  • [Sales Units - Refined and Specialty Oils]: Volumes in the Refined and Specialty Oils segment increased by 3% to 9,134 thousand metric tons, supported by higher demand in Asia and expanded capacity in North America.
  • [Sales Units - Milling]: The Milling segment reported a 9% increase in volumes to 3,703 thousand metric tons, driven by higher activity in both South American and North American regions.
  • [New Production Launches]: The company expanded capacity at the Avondale refinery in North America, contributing to increased volumes in the Refined and Specialty Oils segment.
  • [Future Outlook]: The company anticipates continued impacts from global supply and demand factors on its Agribusiness products, with potential imbalances in oilseed processing capacity affecting operational decisions. Additionally, the integration of Viterra's network is expected to influence future operations.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Acquisition of Viterra: Bunge Global SA has undertaken the acquisition of Viterra, which is expected to enhance its network of agricultural storage, processing, and transport assets.
  • Sale of BP Bunge Bioenergia: The company has completed the sale of its 50% interest in BP Bunge Bioenergia, a joint venture with BP, to streamline its operations and focus on core segments.
  • Acquisition Debt Financing: Bunge has secured $8 billion in acquisition debt financing to support the Viterra acquisition, demonstrating a strategic focus on expanding its operational capabilities and market presence.
  • Share Repurchase Program: Bunge has expanded its share repurchase program by an additional $500 million, bringing total authorizations to $2.7 billion. As of December 31, 2024, the company repurchased 12,150,763 shares for $1.1 billion.
  • Senior Notes Issuance: The company issued three tranches of Senior Notes for an aggregate principal amount of $2 billion to support its acquisition strategy.
  • Liquidity Position: Bunge has maintained a strong liquidity position with $5,665 million in unused committed borrowing capacity and has managed its debt profile by reducing interest expenses and increasing cash and cash equivalents.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Bunge plans to continue its strategic focus on expanding its core operations and enhancing its market position through the integration of Viterra. The company intends to make capital expenditures in the range of $1.5 billion to $1.7 billion in 2025, prioritizing growth and productivity projects, particularly in the oilseeds platform, biofuels, and plant-based proteins. Bunge also aims to maintain a strong balance sheet and liquidity position to support its strategic initiatives and capital management activities.

Challenges and Risks

  • Volatility in Agribusiness and Food Industry: Bunge operates in a highly volatile industry where factors such as weather, governmental policies, and global economic conditions significantly affect the availability and market prices of agricultural commodities.
  • Energy, Transportation, and Logistics Costs: The company's profitability is influenced by energy, transportation, and logistics costs, which are subject to fluctuations.
  • Integration of Viterra: The integration of Viterra's network presents operational challenges, as the company must effectively manage the integration of agricultural storage, processing, and transport assets.
  • Sale of BP Bunge Bioenergia: The sale of Bunge's 50% interest in BP Bunge Bioenergia introduces risks related to the loss of earnings from this joint venture and the need to manage the transition effectively.
  • Foreign Currency Exchange Rates: Fluctuations in local currencies compared to the U.S. dollar can impact the company's consolidated financial statements, affecting both income and cash flows.
  • Income Taxes: Bunge operates in multiple tax jurisdictions with varying statutory rates and complex tax laws, posing risks related to income taxes.
  • Market Risks: Bunge is exposed to market risks associated with agricultural commodity prices, transportation costs, foreign currency exchange rates, interest rates, and energy costs. The company actively manages these risks through derivative instruments and a global governance framework.
  • Credit and Counterparty Risks: Credit and counterparty risks are significant, especially during periods of economic downturns or significant price volatility. The company monitors these risks through exposure reporting and credit analysis.
  • Commodities Risk: Agricultural commodities are subject to unpredictable price fluctuations, and Bunge uses derivative contracts to manage exposure to adverse price movements.
  • Ocean Freight and Energy Costs: Ocean freight and energy costs are significant risks, with market prices varying based on supply and demand, global economic conditions, and inflationary pressures. Bunge uses financial derivatives to hedge portions of these costs.
  • Currency Risk: Currency risk is managed through foreign exchange derivatives, but the potential loss from adverse changes in exchange rates remains a concern.
  • Interest Rate Risk: Changes in interest rates can impact the company's borrowing costs and financial position, as Bunge has debt in both fixed and floating rate instruments.
  • Inflation Risk: Inflation risk affects labor and overhead costs, and while Bunge has historically managed to recover these through sales price increases, future recovery is uncertain.
  • Risk Management: Bunge's management is focused on mitigating these risks through strategic use of financial instruments, careful monitoring of market conditions, and maintaining a strong governance framework to oversee risk management activities.

SEC Filing: Bunge Global SA [ BG ] - 10-K - Feb. 20, 2025

data.title: Robbins LLP Reminds MPWR Investors with Large Losses that Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. is Being Sued by Stockholders; Contact Us for More Information
data.description:
SAN DIEGO, Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Robbins LLP reminds stockholders that a class action was filed on behalf of all purchasers of Monolithic Power Systems Inc. common stock between February 8, 2024 and November 8, 2024. Monolithic is a provider of power management components used in electronic systems.

For more information, submit a form, email attorney Aaron Dumas, Jr., or give us a call at (800) 350-6003.

The Allegations: Robbins LLP is Investigating Allegations that Monolithic Power Systems Inc. (MPWR) Misled Investors Regarding its Products Performance and Quality Control Issues

According to the complaint, during the class period, defendants failed to disclose: (a) that Monolithic’s voltage regulator modules and power management integrated circuits were suffering from significant performance and quality control issues; (b) that the defects listed in (a), above, had, in turn, negatively impacted the performance of certain products offered by Nvidia in which such products were used; (c) that Monolithic had failed to adequately address and resolve known issues affecting the performance of the power management solutions the Company supplied to Nvidia; (d) that Monolithic’s relationship with Nvidia – the Company’s most important customer – had been irreparably damaged due to the significant performance and quality control problems affecting the products it supplied to Nvidia and the Company’s failure to adequately address such issues, as listed in (a)-(c), above; and that as a result of (a)-(d), above, Monolithic was acutely exposed to material undisclosed risks of significant business, financial, and reputational harm. When the truth was revealed, the price of Monolithic stock declined more than 30% from its class period high of more than $959 per share to approximately $647 per share by the end of the class period.

What Now: You may be eligible to participate in the class action against Monolithic Power Systems Inc. Shareholders who want to serve as lead plaintiff for the class must file papers with the court by April 7, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party who acts on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation. You do not have to participate in the case to be eligible for a recovery. If you choose to take no action, you can remain an absent class member. For more information, click here.

All representation is on a contingency fee basis. Shareholders pay no fees or expenses.  

About Robbins LLP: A recognized leader in shareholder rights litigation, the attorneys and staff of Robbins LLP have been dedicated to helping shareholders recover losses, improve corporate governance structures, and hold company executives accountable for their wrongdoing since 2002.

To be notified if a class action against Monolithic Power Systems Inc. settles or to receive free alerts when corporate executives engage in wrongdoing, sign up for Stock Watch today.

Attorney Advertising. Past results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

  • Contact:Aaron Dumas, Jr. Robbins LLP5060 Shoreham Pl., Ste. 300San Diego, CA 92122adumas@robbinsllp.com(800) 350-6003www.robbinsllp.com
  • https://www.facebook.com/RobbinsLLP/https://www.linkedin.com/company/robbins-llp/

A photo accompanying this announcement is available at https://www.globenewswire.com/NewsRoom/AttachmentNg/dd4bbd89-5e09-467d-8d83-1637e1574f45

Monolithic Power Systems Inc.

data.title: Genesis Energy Ltd. 1H Rev NZD1.761B
Genesis Energy Ltd. 1H Net NZD70.3M
Yields dip as Bessent says no near-term plans to change debt sales
data.description:
Key points:
  • Fed meeting minutes raise hopes for earlier end to QT
  • Initial jobless claims rise slightly in latest week

U.S. Treasury yields edged lower on Thursday after comments from the top Treasury official eased concerns of any looming increase in the size of longer-dated debt auctions.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said a near-term change is not planned given hurdles, including the Federal Reserve’s quantitative tightening program, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday.

“It further kicks the can down the road for a higher issuance, maybe the end of this year, beginning of 2026 at the earliest, but we already knew that from the last refunding announcement,” said Will Compernolle, macro strategist at FHN Financial in Chicago.

Traders had been expecting Bessent to issue more longer-dated debt after formerly criticizing the Treasury under his Democratic predecessor, Janet Yellen, for relying heavily on short-term debt issuance.

But at its latest refunding announcement, the first under Bessent, the Treasury said it expects to keep most of its debt issuance plans unchanged for at least the next few quarters.

Bessent’s comments followed the release on Wednesday of minutes from the latest Federal Reserve meeting that showed policymakers discussed slowing or pausing the Fed's quantitative tightening program.

The Fed has been letting bonds roll off its balance sheet without replacement since June 2022. Slowing or pausing the program may reduce the amount of debt the Treasury Department needs to offer.

Fed officials flagged the challenge of getting a clear read on market liquidity as the Treasury wrangles with the reinstatement of the U.S. debt ceiling on January 2, which affects how it can manage cash.

Some analysts say the Fed may now end the program earlier than previously expected.

"It may not make sense to pause QT, say, the March or May meetings, only to briefly restart and end asset roll-offs in September or October. Instead, we think the Fed is more likely to end QT early than pause or taper," Barclays analyst Joseph Abate said in a report on Thursday.

The Treasury saw solid demand for a $9 billion sale of 30-year Treasury inflation-protected securities on Thursday.

The debt sold at a high yield of 2.403%, which was the highest yield in a 30-year TIPS auction since the Treasury reintroduced the security in 2010, according to analysts at BMO Capital Markets. Demand was 2.48 times the amount on offer. (USAUCTION57)

Meanwhile, data on Thursday showed that the number of Americans filing new applications for unemployment benefits increased moderately last week, suggesting that the labor market remained on solid ground.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes was last down 3.4 basis points on the day at 4.501%.

The two-year note (US2YT=RR) yield fell 0.8 basis point to 4.266%.

The yield curve between two-year and 10-year notes (US2US10=TWEB) flattened by around three basis points to 23 basis points.

St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem on Thursday raised the twin risks of rising inflation expectations and difficult-to-address stagflation in remarks that highlighted the potentially difficult choices facing the U.S. central bank.

Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, in separate remarks on Thursday, gave a tempered view on inflation risks, saying he does not expect the inflation reading the U.S. central bank uses to set its inflation target to be as "sobering" as the previously reported Consumer Price Index figures.

Traders are concerned that tariffs planned by President Donald Trump will add to already sticky inflation.

Market volatility over tariff announcements has fallen in recent weeks, however, as the tariff implementation has been delayed, raising hopes that they are a negotiating tool and may not be as bad as feared.

Trump said on Wednesday he would announce tariffs over the next month or sooner, adding lumber and forest products to previous plans to impose duties on imported cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals.

Developments in discussions for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal are also in focus.

Trump's envoy for the conflict in Ukraine met President Volodymyr Zelenskiy in Kyiv on Thursday, but there was no immediate word on whether their talks had helped smooth over an unprecedented wartime rift between the once firm allies.

data.title: Ferrari Plans to Increase Dividend From Year Ago
data.description:
Ferrari said Thursday its board plans to recommend to its shareholders a dividend of 2.986 euros ($3.13) per share, up about 22% from a year ago.

If the distribution is approved by shareholders at its annual general meeting on April 16, the dividend will be paid out on May 6 to shareholders of record April 23, the company said.

data.title: New Zealand's Fonterra expects to meet upper half of fiscal 2025 forecast, hints at strong dividend
data.description:
New Zealand's Fonterra said on Friday that it expects earnings to meet the upper half of its forecast range of 40 to 60 New Zealand cents per share for the fiscal 2025.

The dairy giant also expects to to be in a position to pay a strong interim dividend.

data.title: Fed's Musalem flags risk of rising inflation expectations and stagflation
data.description:
Key points:
  • Musalem warns of the risks inflation could remain high
  • Musalem needs confidence inflation waning to support more rate cuts

St. Louis Federal Reserve President Alberto Musalem on Thursday raised the twin risks of rising inflation expectations and difficult-to-address stagflation, in remarks that highlighted the potentially difficult choices facing the U.S. central bank.

While many of his colleagues say they regard inflation expectations as anchored, Musalem said he was concerned recent data showed they may be rising - a development that might force the Fed onto a more restrictive path.

"In the current environment, the stakes are higher than they would be if inflation was at or below target," Musalem said in a speech given before the Economic Club of New York. "The risk that inflation expectations could become unanchored is higher than it would be if the economy was operating with slack and if consumers and businesses had not recently experienced a period of high inflation."

While he said he still feels inflation will converge to the Fed's 2% target, "market and some survey measures indicate that near-term expectations of inflation have risen notably over the past three months," Musalem said. If inflation does get stuck at current above-target levels or expectations do rise, "a more restrictive path of monetary policy relative to the baseline path might be appropriate."

In comments to reporters after his speech, Musalem said "a durable convergence" of inflation back to the 2% target would help give him confidence the Fed could cut rates again, but he offered no timetable for that to happen.

He also told reporters "my baseline scenario is one where inflation continues to converge towards 2% provided monetary policy remains modestly restrictive and it will take time” for that to happen, while adding "I see the risks of inflation staying above the (2%) target as skewed to the upside."

Musalem's warnings spoke to the possible complications surrounding the Fed's main narrative of falling inflation and eventual further rate cuts, an outlook that has remained the baseline even as officials acknowledge the possible impact on prices of new Trump administration import taxes and immigration rules.

That remains Musalem's core outlook as well, with monetary policy remaining restrictive "until inflation convergence is assured."

But coming policy shifts "could materially affect the path of the economy," he said. "The risks of inflation stalling above 2% or moving higher seem skewed to the upside ... An alternative and plausible scenario in which inflation ceases to converge, or rises, at the same time the labor market weakens must also be considered."

The combination of slowing growth and high inflation is a worst-case scenario for central bankers, leaving the Fed with tension between its employment and inflation goals and forced to choose which one to prioritize.

These Stocks Are Moving the Most Today: Carvana, Vimeo, Alibaba, Wayfair, Palantir, D-Wave Quantum, and More — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Stocks were sliding amid a market-wide selloff Thursday after the S&P 500 closed at a record high in the previous session. The S&P and the Nasdaq Composite were both off 0.6% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 1.3%.

These stocks were making moves:

Carvana stock fell 14% to $242.70, even after the auto dealer reported fourth-quarter sales and earnings that topped expectations. Carvana said it "expects significant growth" in retail unit sales and adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization in 2025, but did not provide specific guidance. D.A. Davidson analyst Michael Baker lifted his price target to $260 from $220 and maintained a Hold rating, arguing the pricey shares could keep gaining if certain conditions are met.

Walmart fell 6.4% after supplying weak guidance for fiscal 2026, the period ending next January. For the full year, the retailer expects net sales to increase by 3% to 4%. Analysts were expecting around 4% growth from the previous year. Adjusted earnings also came in below expectations, with the $2.55 midpoint of the guidance range missing the $2.77 Wall Street was looking for.

Wayfair fell 1.1%. The online furniture retailer posted revenue of $3.12 billion for the fourth quarter, topping the $3.066 billion analysts expected. However, adjusting for one-time charges, the company reported a loss of 25 cents per share, more than double the adjusted loss from the prior year.

Vimeo stock plunged 19% after the video hosting and sharing platform reported a decline in profit after its number of subscribers fell in the fourth quarter. Earnings of 1 cent a share came in below analysts' calls for 3 cents a share. The company also guided for lower revenue in the current quarter.

Palantir Technologies fell 7.1%, extending losses from Wednesday, when the stock closed down 10%. The decline followed reports of potential U.S. defense budget cuts, and came after CEO Alex Karp adopted a new trading plan to sell up to 9,975,000 shares of the company.

Super Micro Computer declined 0.9%, on track to snap its latest winning streak. The stock closed up 8% at $60.25 on Wednesday, extending the rally to five days. The stock's record-longest rally was 12 days finishing in December 2013.

U.S.-listed shares of Alibaba were up 8.8% after the online marketplace reported financial results for the December quarter. The company posted revenue of 280.15 billion yuan ($38.38 billion), an 8% increase from the prior year and was roughly in line with the 279.34 billion yuan analysts forecast. Management said the company showed substantial progress in its "user first, AI-driven" strategies during the period.

Microsoft rose 0.9%. The gains came after the company unveiled its Majorana 1 quantum processing unit, which is part of a research effort and not commercially available. Microsoft said it was one step closer to building quantum computers "capable of solving meaningful, industrial-scale problems."

Shares of quantum stocks were mostly rising on the heels of the Microsoft announcement. D-Wave Quantum was up 9.8%, Rigetti Computing was up 4%, and Quantum Computing was up 5.4%. IonQ fell 1.1% after climbing in premarket trading.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

Ebitda or Adjusted Ebitda? Demystifying Valuations in RIA M&A. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Harris Baltch

In a slightly slowing but still hot RIA M&A landscape, we are seeing an increasing number of private-equity firms favoring adjusted Ebitda as a valuation method.

Part of the reason is that adjusted Ebitda can provide a clearer and more standardized picture of a company's core operating performance, enabling better comparisons across different companies and industries and facilitating more informed investment decisions. We have also noticed that RIAs can be more successful selling their firms when using this metric.

Both Ebitda (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) and adjusted Ebitda have their strengths, and both are subject to distortions and anomalies. Given this, it is important for all parties in a merger, acquisition, or sale to understand the differences.

Ebitda is an alternative, non-GAAP, and unlevered measure of profitability to net income. Ebitda shows how well a company is managing its day-to-day operations, and as such is a fair indicator of a business's current state and potential. Indeed, in some cases it can be a better indicator than gross profit or net income.

However, Ebitda metrics can also produce misleading results. When firms go to market and position themselves as platforms for private equity, it isn't uncommon for partners and owners to change compensation levels, allow advisors who aren't owners to trade in a certain amount of compensation for equity, remove certain non-core expenses, and even equitize support staff in some way to increase Ebitda.

Adjusted Ebitda is calculated by normalizing Ebitda for certain nonoperating and one-time expenses to more appropriately ballpark pretax cash flow from operations. This way, a company's value can be estimated based on transactions that have taken place between other companies of similar size and characteristics.

So how much value should be placed on these two metrics, especially during M&A due diligence?

The main advantage of using adjusted Ebitda is that it provides a cleaner and more relevant picture of a company's operating health and performance by removing nonoperating expenses that may not be directly related to a company's core business.

Another advantage is that it is easier to compare the performance of companies within the same industry, regardless of their capital structure and tax situations. Adjusted Ebitda is also commonly used in valuation multiples to provide a standardized metric for assessing value across different companies.

Yet there is no universal standard for adjusted Ebitda, and the way it is calculated can vary from company to company. Therefore, adjusted Ebitda tends to be more subjective than Ebitda, and companies may adjust or manipulate the metric differently using various assumptions. By removing or understating certain expenses, for example, fluffy Ebitda metrics may provide an incomplete or inaccurate picture of a company's financial performance and distort valuation.

Acceptable add-backs. Therefore, while adjusted Ebitda can be a useful tool for evaluating a company's financial performance it should be used in conjunction with other financial metrics and should not be relied on as the sole indicator of a company's financial health. Acquirers should scrutinize a target's financial statements and not take every figure at face value. For their part, sellers should be transparent about how they adjust and provide details on each component of modifications. To accurately assess true financial performance, both buyers and sellers often make certain legitimate adjustments, or add-backs, to the Ebitda figure.

Nonrecurring expenses, for example, are one-time costs that are unlikely to occur again in the future. As such, they can be adjusted or added back to Ebitda to provide a more accurate representation of a company's ongoing profitability. Examples include litigation gains or losses, gains or losses on the sale of assets, severance costs, and legal or consulting fees related to a transaction relating to acquisition or regulatory matters. In these cases, adjustments are made to account for the extraordinary nature of these events and provide a more accurate representation of a business's ongoing profitability.

Another example relates to owner compensation. In some RIAs, total owner expenses (including compensation, benefits and T&E) might exceed market rates due to personal expenses flowing through the business. Some of these perks — such as country club memberships, donations, or luxury vehicles that aren't essential for business operations, may be added back to provide a clearer financial picture.

On the other hand, understating replacement owner compensation can distort the true operational performance of an RIA firm. For example, if a firm owner is taking a minimal salary due to their ownership stake and is primarily compensated through distributions, it would be inappropriate to continue using this low salary figure in the adjusted Ebitda calculation — especially when preparing to sell the business.

Instead, the firm should account for the market rate salary that would be necessary to hire a replacement for the owner. Failing to do so could artificially inflate Ebitda, leading to misleading valuations.

Quality of earnings. Quality of earnings. For buyers, one aspect of due diligence involves analyzing adjusted Ebitda to reconcile whether a company's earnings are what they are purported to be. What is recurring revenue and what isn't? Is the seller attempting to inflate the value of a business by not taking a salary? Does the target provide vague descriptions and rationales behind certain adjustments?

In this effort, a quality of earnings (QoE) analysis — a financial accounting process that evaluates a company's financial performance by removing distortions and anomalies from its earnings in order to assess a company's operational efficiency — may be useful in identifying red flags.

Overall, having an investment banker in your corner to act as a resource and a guide in an M&A due diligence situation can be tremendously helpful for buyers and sellers alike.

Harris Baltch is head of investment banking at Dynasty Financial Partners. Prior to joining Dynasty, he spent nearly a decade at UBS Investment Bank where he was an executive director covering the asset and wealth management industry in the firm's Financial Institutions Group.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

USDJPY stretches to the swing area and the 50% retracement
data.description:
USDJPY broke below its yearly low and February low at 150.943, accelerating downside momentum. The decline extended to 149.39, aligning with the upper boundary of a key swing area dating back to mid-August 2024 (149.08 - 149.39). This level also coincides with the 50% retracement of the September-to-January uptrend at 149.221, making it a crucial technical support zone. The price bounced off this area and currently trades around 149.78.

Today's selloff covered 203 pips, well above the 20-day average of 152 pips, indicating an oversold move that likely contributed to buyers stepping in early. The key question now is: What could slow the downtrend and give sellers reason to hesitate?

Key Resistance Levels for a Recovery:

  • 150.03 – The 200-bar moving average (5-minute chart). USDJPY has breached the 100-bar MA intraday (blue line on the chart below) currently at 149.72 today, but remains below the 200 bar MA key level at 150.03. A sustained break above the 200 bar MA on that chart is needed to signal the first small victory for buyers.
  • 150.17 - 150.25 – A key swing area that must be reclaimed to build bullish momentum. See red numbered circles and yellow area on the chart below)
  • 150.321 – The 38.2% retracement of the latest downside move, which would further strengthen buyer confidence.

As long as USDJPY remains below these resistance zones, sellers remain in control The buyers may be more in play (not getting overwhelmed with trend selling). A failure to break back above 150.033 would likely keep downside pressure intact, with 149.39 and 149.08 as critical support levels.

Nvidia Stock Rises. Why the Chip Maker Got a Price-Target Upgrade. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark and George Glover

Nvidia stock was edging higher Thursday, with investors in wait-and-see mode ahead of the chip maker's earnings next week.

Shares rose 0.5% to $139.86 in early trading. The benchmark S&P 500 index was down 0.3%.

Nvidia is due to publish earnings for its fourth quarter, which ended Jan. 26, next Wednesday. Investors are hopeful for another profit surge after Big Tech companies including Amazon.com and Google-parent Alphabet guided for a rise in capital spending this year, suggesting they'll carry on pouring money into the company's artificial-intelligence hardware.

Google Cloud said Thursday that it was offering a preview of cloud-computing services powered by Nvidia's GB200 NV liquid-cooled rack systems, with 72 graphics-processing units networked together.

KeyBanc analyst John Vinh, who rates Nvidia shares Overweight, raised his price target on the stock to $190 from $180 Wednesday, citing higher-than-expected supply of Nvidia's Blackwell B200 chips.

Vinh's price target implies the stock can rise 36% from its level as of Wednesday's close. It is based on a price-to-earnings multiple of 38 times the analyst's forecast for Nvidia's fiscal 2026 earnings.

"We expect Nvidia to report strong F4Q [fiscal fourth quarter] results, which we anticipate will solidly beat, and to guide F1Q conservatively and moderately higher than consensus," Vinh wrote.

Vinh expects Nvidia to report fiscal fourth-quarter revenue of $38.2 billion and earnings per share of 85 cents, ahead of consensus estimates of $38.0 billion and 84 cents. For its first quarter, he expects revenue of $43.0 billion and earnings per share of 94 cents.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com and George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Walmart's Comp Sales Growth Slowdown Drives Rivals Lower — Market Talk
data.description: Walmart's warning of slower sales gains, together with slowdown comparable sales growth during the holiday quarter, is sparking concerns on Wall Street. The retail giant's 4.6% growth in U.S. comparable sales in the fourth quarter from a 5.3% increase in the third quarter shows lower prices haven't been enough to drive sentiment higher among Americans. "We're growing profit faster than sales," CEO Douglas McMillon says on a call with analysts, noting that Walmart has runway to scale our higher-margin businesses like membership, marketplace and advertising. Its wholesale business, Sam's Club, also saw a sequential deceleration in comparable sales in the period, driving Costco Wholesale shares 2.4% lower. Shares of Amazon, Target and Kroger are down between 1.5% and 2%, while Walmart drops 6.5%. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

IACMI Open House Celebrates 10 Years of Innovation, New Headquarters, and Cutting-edge Composites Laboratory
data.description:
KNOXVILLE, Tenn., Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — IACMI–The Composites Institute® celebrated today the opening of its new national headquarters in Knoxville, Tenn., located in Innovation South at the University of Tennessee (UT) Research Park at Cherokee Farm.

The IACMI offices are co-located with the new, expanded UT Fibers and Composites Manufacturing Facility (FCMF), a 44,000 square-foot, state-of-the-art laboratory. This lab will serve as a pivotal center for advanced composites and manufacturing, driving research, commercialization, and workforce training to strengthen U.S. competitiveness.

“Innovation South provides a strategic location for IACMI that will serve as a hub in the Southeast for regional collaboration in cutting-edge composites and advanced manufacturing,” said Chad Duty, IACMI CEO. “This new location, along with FCMF, offers exceptional opportunities for public-private partnerships, providing IACMI members and industry leaders with access to innovative research, world-class facilities, and shared office space in Knoxville.”

Prior to the move to Innovation South, IACMI, established in 2015 by the Department of Energy, had offices near Hardin Valley. For 10 years, IACMI has accelerated the use of advanced composites – which are lighter, stronger, corrosion resistant and energy efficient – in transportation, energy and infrastructure applications. A leader in workforce development, the institute has provided hands-on advanced manufacturing training for more than 5,100 individuals and engaged more than 18,000 students in STEM outreach.

“Innovation South enables us to develop materials-by-design solutions that will reduce the cost, energy, and manufacturing time of next-generation products,” said Uday Vaidya, IACMI Chief Technology Officer and the UT-Oak Ridge National Laboratory Governor’s Chair for Advanced Composites Manufacturing. “It will be a place to conduct research and development at scale and transition innovative ideas and concepts to field commercial applications. This is unique to the East Tennessee ecosystem.”

Some of the equipment in this new facility includes:

  • Michelman Sizing line
  • High speed Wabash Compression Presses -150 Ton, 100 Ton, 500 Ton
  • RocTool induction heating system with custom 18”x18” tool for rapid cycle time processing of thermoplastic composites
  • Automated Tape Placement system
  • Natural fiber matmaking and spinning line
  • Composite Recycling
  • Vacuum Assisted Resin Transfer Molding (VARTM)

The facility will serve as a training hub for students from K-12, technical colleges, universities, and industry professionals, offering hands-on learning experiences in cutting-edge composites and manufacturing. This focus on workforce development ensures that Tennessee’s current and future workforce gains the critical skills needed to meet the growing demand in advanced manufacturing industries.

About IACMI

The Institute for Advanced Composites Manufacturing Innovation, or IACMI–The Composites Institute®, is dedicated to securing U.S. global leadership in advanced manufacturing by connecting people, ideas, and technology. IACMI is a 170-plus member community of industry, academic institutions, and government organizations working to enhance U.S. manufacturing competitiveness, with a strong focus on technology, commercialization and workforce development. Established in 2015 by the Department of Energy, IACMI is one of 18 Manufacturing USA Institutes collaborating to accelerate new technology, create new products, reduce costs and risks, and equip the workforce with future-ready skills. IACMI also partners with the Department of Defense to scale up industry-driven job skills and revitalize American manufacturing. Based in Knoxville, Tennessee, IACMI is managed by the Collaborative Composite Solutions Corporation, a not-for-profit organization established by The University of Tennessee Research Foundation.

For more photos visit: IACMI Open House Photos.

Contact: Margaret Slattery

mslattery@iacmi.org

data.title: CBOT corn stabilizes a day after hitting 16-month high
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade corn futures finished nearly unchanged on Thursday as the market took a breather after climbing to a 16-month high during the previous session, traders said.

  • Traders are looking for direction from developments in U.S. trade policy, farmers' planting intentions or global weather forecasts.

  • Expectations for an increase in U.S. corn plantings this spring continued to hang over the market, as the crop looks more profitable to produce than soybeans, analysts said.

  • Agricultural lender CoBank projected that U.S. farmers will plant 94.55 million acres to corn in 2025, up about 4% from 2024.

  • The International Grains Council cut its forecast for 2024/25 global production, largely reflecting a diminished outlook for crops in Brazil and Argentina.

  • CBOT March corn (CH25) rose 1/2 cent to end at $4.98 per bushel.

data.title: Security Federal Announces Cash Dividend Increase
data.description:
AIKEN, S.C., Feb. 20, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Security Federal Corporation, parent company of Security Federal Bank, is pleased to announce that a quarterly dividend of $0.15 per share will be paid on or about March 15, 2025, to shareholders of record as of February 28. 2025. This increased dividend represents an increase of $0.01, or 7.1%, over the previous regular quarterly dividend.   

This is the one hundred thirty-seventh consecutive quarterly dividend to shareholders since the Bank’s conversion in October of 1987 from a mutual to a stock form of ownership. The dividend was declared as a result of the Bank’s continued profitability.

Security Federal Bank has nineteen full-service branch locations in Aiken, Ballentine, Clearwater, Columbia, Graniteville, Langley, Lexington, North Augusta, Ridge Spring, Wagener and West Columbia, South Carolina and Augusta and Evans, Georgia. A full range of financial services, including trust and investments, are provided by the Bank, and insurance services are provided by the Bank’s wholly owned subsidiary, Security Federal Insurance, Inc.

Security Federal Corporation common stock is traded on the Over-the-Counter Bulletin Board under the symbol SFDL.

data.title: The stock market looks good from the outside - but inside it's another story
data.description:
By Mark Hulbert

The percentage of unprofitable publicly traded U.S. companies is rising

One in eight large-cap companies and 32% of small-caps - about one in three - are unprofitable.

The U.S. stock market looks good from the outside, but inside it's decaying.

It's easy to overlook this internal deterioration as the major market averages are reaching new all-time highs. But the numbers don't lie: An increasing number of publicly traded companies are unprofitable. This trend can't continue indefinitely.

Consider the chart above, courtesy of data from Finomial, the fintech company. The chart shows, for both large-cap and small-cap stocks, the percentage that is unprofitable at any given time. Notice that for each group, the trend over the past two decades is for an increasing share to be unprofitable. Currently, 12% of large-cap companies - roughly one in eight - and 32% of small-caps - about one in three - are unprofitable, despite the U.S. economy being in its 16th year of expansion (overlooking the two-month recession that accompanied the COVID-19 economic lockdown in early 2020).

The unprofitable firms are concentrated among the small caps. The largest companies, in contrast, which have increasingly dominated the market, are more consistently profitable. Many bulls insist that the trends in this chart are not all that alarming.

I'm not so sure. Even though the megacap stocks that dominate the market may be more consistently profitable than the small caps, they still can stumble badly. A market that's dependent on just a few megacaps is vulnerable if several of them stumble at the same time.

If you had any doubt about the market's vulnerability in this regard, consider the largest drawdowns experienced since 2021 by any of the stocks that are part of the "Magnificent Seven" (or their predecessor group, "FAANG"). The market has been fortunate that these stocks didn't simultaneously experience their drawdowns, but there is no guarantee that the market will be so lucky in the coming years.

Skewed p/e ratios

A less-appreciated consequence of the trend toward more widespread unprofitability is how it biases the price/earnings ratio to make the stock market appear to be more reasonably priced than it really is. When a company is losing money, its P/E ratio is negative. When calculating the marketwide P/E ratio, therefore, many data providers simply ignore unprofitable companies - even though there is a process, though more complicated, to incorporate these losses into the calculation.

To appreciate how much the P/E ratio can be biased by ignoring unprofitable companies, consider the Russell 2000 Index RUT, perhaps the most widely-cited benchmark for the small- and midcap sectors of the market. When focusing only on profitable companies, the index's trailing 12-month P/E ratio is 16.57, which on the surface seems reasonable. When properly incorporating unprofitable companies' losses in the calculation, the Russell 2000's P/E more than doubles to 33.89 - in or approaching overvaluation territory.

A healthy stock market is one in which not only are a large percentage of companies profitable, but this percentage is growing. Neither seems to be the case currently.

Mark Hulbert is a regular contributor to MarketWatch. His Hulbert Ratings tracks investment newsletters that pay a flat fee to be audited. He can be reached at mark@hulbertratings.com

More: U.S. stocks are back at record highs. Is it too late to chase the bull market?

Plus: The biggest U.S. stocks haven't been this expensive since the dot-com era. That's making investors nervous.

-Mark Hulbert

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: HUMANA INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Humana Inc., a leading health and well-being company, has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance over the past year. The report highlights significant growth in revenue, strategic initiatives aimed at focusing on Medicare Advantage, and the challenges faced in a competitive and regulated industry.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $117,761 million, reflecting a 10.7% increase from the previous year primarily due to higher per member Medicare premiums and membership growth in Medicare Advantage and state-based contracts.

Income from Operations: $2,562 million, a decrease of 36.2% from the previous year, primarily due to higher benefit expenses and lower favorable prior period medical claims reserve development.

Net Income: $1,214 million, a decrease of 51.1% from the previous year, significantly impacted by put/call valuation adjustments, charges associated with value creation initiatives, and impairment charges.

Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $9.98, a decrease of 50.1% from the previous year, reflecting the same factors impacting net income.

Benefit Ratio: 89.8%, an increase of 2.5 percentage points from the previous year, primarily due to elevated Medicare Advantage and state-based contracts medical cost trends.

Operating Cost Ratio: 11.8%, a decrease of 0.7 percentage points from the previous year, driven by scale efficiencies and administrative cost efficiencies resulting from value creation initiatives.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The Insurance segment, which includes Medicare and state-based contracts, generated significant revenue growth, primarily driven by higher per member Medicare premiums and membership growth in Medicare Advantage and state-based contracts.

Geographical Performance: Florida remains a key market, with approximately 924,800 individual Medicare Advantage members, contributing to 19% of the individual Medicare Advantage premiums revenue.

Sales Units: The company reported a total insurance medical membership of approximately 16.3 million as of December 31, 2024, with notable growth in individual Medicare Advantage membership, which increased by 252,900 members or 4.7% from the previous year.

New Product Launches: The company continues to expand its Medicare Advantage offerings, including Dual-Eligible Special Needs Plans (D-SNP), which saw a net increase of 65,800 members or 7.6% in 2024.

New Production Launches: The CenterWell segment expanded its primary care operations, with 344 primary care clinics and approximately 1,000 primary care providers, serving around 390,500 patients.

Future Outlook: For 2025, the company anticipates a net membership decline in individual Medicare Advantage offerings of approximately 550,000 members, while expecting growth in state-based contracts by 175,000 to 250,000 members.

Operational Efficiency: The company achieved administrative cost efficiencies through value creation initiatives, contributing to a decrease in the operating cost ratio from 12.5% in 2023 to 11.8% in 2024.

Strategic Partnerships: The CenterWell segment's strategic partnership with Welsh, Carson, Anderson & Stowe (WCAS) continues to support the expansion of senior-focused primary care centers, with 133 clinics operating under the partnership as of December 31, 2024.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Humana Inc. announced its planned exit from the Employer Group Commercial Medical Products business to focus on its Medicare Advantage business and healthcare services expansion. This strategic decision is part of a broader initiative to drive value through cost savings, productivity improvements, and value acceleration from previous investments. The company recorded charges related to asset impairments and severance as part of these initiatives.

Capital Management: Humana engaged in several capital management activities, including issuing $1.3 billion of 5.375% unsecured senior notes due 2031 and $1.0 billion of 5.750% unsecured senior notes due 2054, with net proceeds used for general corporate purposes and debt repayment. The company also repurchased $803 million of its own shares and paid $431 million in dividends to stockholders. Additionally, Humana entered into a securities lending program and an uncommitted receivables purchase facility, providing net proceeds of $418 million and $123 million, respectively.

Future Outlook: Humana plans to finalize its exit from the Employer Group Commercial Medical Products business by the first half of 2025. The company anticipates continued focus on Medicare Advantage and state-based contracts, with expected membership growth in these areas. Humana also intends to maintain significant levels of aggregate excess statutory capital and surplus in its state-regulated operating subsidiaries, ensuring adequate resources for ongoing operations, regulatory requirements, and future expansion opportunities.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: Humana Inc. operates in a highly competitive industry with significant reliance on government contracts, particularly Medicare and Medicaid, which account for a substantial portion of its revenue. The company faces challenges in maintaining and expanding its market share due to intense competition and the potential for new entrants, especially in the Medicare program. Additionally, the planned exit from the Employer Group Commercial Medical Products business by 2025 indicates a strategic shift that may impact revenue streams.

Pricing and Cost Management: The company must accurately design and price its products to cover healthcare costs. Failure to do so could materially affect profitability. Factors such as medical cost inflation, increased use of medical services, and changes in government-mandated benefits pose risks to cost management.

Regulatory Risks: Changes in government policies, particularly those affecting Medicare Advantage and Prescription Drug Plans, could impact profitability. Legislative actions that reduce premium payments or increase costs could have adverse effects.

Operational Risks: The company's ability to implement clinical initiatives and manage operating expenses is crucial. Failure to effectively manage these could adversely affect financial results.

Cybersecurity and Data Integrity: Humana's reliance on information systems and third-party service providers exposes it to cybersecurity risks. Breaches could lead to significant regulatory fines, reputational damage, and operational disruptions.

Legal and Compliance Risks: The company is involved in various legal actions and investigations, which could result in substantial damages or changes in business practices if resolved unfavorably.

Market Risks: The decline in Medicare Advantage Star Ratings for 2025 poses a risk to future quality bonus payments and could affect revenues and cash flows.

Management's Focus: Management is focused on executing strategic initiatives, particularly in the Medicare segment, to drive growth. The company is investing in its integrated care delivery model and expanding its CenterWell segment to enhance healthcare services. However, the concentration of revenues in Medicare products intensifies risks associated with regulatory changes and market competition.

Market Volatility: Humana is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations and changes in the securities and credit markets, which could affect the value of its investment portfolio and investment income. The company monitors these risks and believes it has adequate resources to manage them, but ongoing market volatility could impact financial performance.
